page: Microsoft Azure E104ids_v5 is an isolated virtual machine instance belonging to the EIDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 104 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs derived from 52 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The system is equipped with 672.0 GB of system memory, yielding 6.46 GB of RAM per vCPU. Local storage is bundled, providing 3891 GB of local disk capacity, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. No GPU accelerators or complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core capabilities and memory performance. The instance achieved a strong multi-core stress-ng CPU score of 100881.58 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 17965.0, contrasted with average single-core performance across stress-ng and Geekbench tests. Memory bandwidth is highly rated, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 5422.71 GB/sec and uncached large block read speeds at 333.55 GB/sec. Cryptographic performance is particularly notable, with the PassMark CPU encryption test placing in the top tier at 92867.76. Database operations and Redis SET operations also demonstrated strong performance, with Redis handling 94.43 million operations per second.

From a resource density perspective, the E104ids_v5 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio alongside substantial bundled local storage, which reduces the need for external storage attachments for high-throughput workloads. The hardware isolation provides dedicated compute resources, ensuring consistent performance. However, single-threaded execution speeds remain average, indicating that workloads relying heavily on single-core clock speed may experience a bottleneck compared to multi-threaded tasks.

This instance is configured for memory-intensive and data-heavy applications. Appropriate workloads include large-scale relational and non-relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, data warehousing, and multi-threaded enterprise applications that benefit from the 672.0 GB memory capacity and 3891 GB of local storage.
description: Microsoft Azure E104ids_v5 is an isolated, memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 104 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs and 672.0 GB of RAM. Running on Microsoft Hyper-V, it features 3891 GB of bundled local storage and supports Premium Storage. Benchmark results show strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 100881.58, and top-tier cryptographic capabilities, highlighted by a PassMark encryption score of 92867.76. Memory bandwidth is highly rated, reaching 5422.71 GB/sec for cached small blocks. While single-core performance is average, the high memory density and substantial local storage make this instance appropriate for memory-intensive databases, large-scale Redis deployments, and multi-threaded enterprise workloads requiring hardware isolation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E104ids_v5 is an isolated memory-optimized instance with 104 vCPUs, 672 GB RAM, and 3891 GB local storage, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E104ids_v5 is an isolated 104-vCPU instance with 672 GB RAM and 3891 GB local storage, optimized for memory-heavy database workloads.
tagline: An isolated, high-memory virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, substantial bundled local storage, and strong multi-threaded database performance.
bullet_points: 104 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8370C processors
672.0 GB of system memory for memory-intensive applications
3891 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier cryptographic performance and strong multi-core execution
Isolated hardware configuration running on Microsoft Hyper-V
Best suited for large-scale databases, Redis, and data warehousing
